items.title: How Trump's tariffs will impact the global economy | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump has announced he is imposing tariffs on the US' three largest trading partners - Canada, Mexico and China - starting on Saturday. 
The White House says it will implement a 25 percent tax on imports from Canada and Mexico, and a 10 percent tax on goods from China. Trump has also threatened the EU with similar measures, but has offered no clear timeline. Trump had pledged the measure to boost local manufacturing in the US, and to pressure its neighbors to do more to stop the flow of migrants and illicit drugs into the country.
Both Canada and Mexico have said they're ready to retaliate. But analysts say the US tax will likely deal a heavy blow to Mexico - Latin America's second-largest economy.

00:00 US imposes tariffs on its bigest trading partners
03:53 DW speaks with Kimberly Clausing at the UCLA school of law
08:31 DW speaks with John Kirton, professor of political science at the University of Toronto

items.title: Investigators search for black box flight recorder from army helicopter | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US investigators are still searching for the black box recorder from the army helicopter in the Washington D.C. air disaster. Authorities believe the airport control tower was understaffed when the helicopter collided with a passenger jet. 67 people were killed. Among the victims were athletes and coaches returning from the US figure-skating championsip in Kansas.

Chapters

items.title: Germany: Lawmakers reject controversial bill intended to reform immigration | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Lawmakers in Germany have rejected a controversial bill on immigration reform. The draft legislation was proposed by the conservative opposition CDU party, and sparked outrage not necessarily because of its content, but because the only chance for it to pass would have been with backing from the far-right Alternative for Germany party. A successful vote would have marked the first time in Germany's post-war history that federal legislation is pushed through with the support of the far right. It did not pass. And still many ask: is the genie now out of the bottle?

Chapters:
0:00 The vote, the consequences, and the other vote
3:34 Matthew Karnitschnig, Euroactiv Editor-in-Chief

items.title: Colombia's anti-drug operations thrown into jeopardy following US deportations | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump has shaken up US policy since taking office at the start of last week. Mass deportations of irregular migrants and the freezing of foreign aid budgets are affecting countries well beyond the US borders. South-American Colombia especially has been at the sharp end of those policies. After the Trump presidency forced the government in BogotĂˇ to accept deported migrants the country's anti-drug operations are now in jeopardy. 

A large part of Colombia's fleet of anti-narcotics helicopters has been grounded after Washington ended aid payments. The aircraft belong to the United States and the US also pays for their maintenance. Colombia is the biggest cocaine producer in the world with most of the drug smuggled to the United States.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Brexit five years on: The reason few are celebrating | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>It's five years to the day that the United Kingdom left the European Union. That happened after a divisive vote that split the electorate down the middle and left the nation divided. The social bruises from that battle may have faded. But it's fair to say this fifth anniversary isn't been marked by huge street parties either. 

Polls say a majority of British people now think Brexit was a mistake. But there's also very little support for rejoining the European Union. Too great was the trauma of the polarization and arguments surrounding the Brexit debate.

Five years on, people and businesses are still wrestling with the economic, social and cultural aftershocks. Here's the CEO of one small business with customers in the EU.

Chapters:
0:00 Brexit, five years on and mostly forgotten?
1:28 Catherine Barnard, Law Professor, Trinity College

items.title: What does this result mean for opposition leader Friedrich Merz and his CDU party? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Lawmakers in Germany have rejected a controversial bill on immigration reform. The draft legislation was proposed by the conservative opposition CDU party, but it sparked outrage because it would have only passed with the support of the far-right Alternative for Germany party. It would have seen the end of family reunification for some migrants, given border police extra powers to assist in deportations. And it would have made limiting immigration a government policy. 

Chapters

items.title: Man trapped in Japan sinkhole | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A large-scale rescue is underway in Japan to save a 74-year-old truck driver trapped in a sinkhole. The operation in Yashio City, Saitama, has entered its fourth day. The sinkhole, initially 10 meters wide and 5 meters deep, has expanded after merging with another one, complicating operations. The incident, which happened during Tuesday's rush hour, has drawn national attention.
